title: Networking Mastery Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the OSI model, protocols, and standard ports.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for establishing, managing, and terminating sessions between applications?
questions.explanation: The Session layer (Layer 5) handles the setup and teardown of communication channels.

questions.question: A MAC address is a physical identifier that is typically burned into the network hardware.
questions.explanation: Correct. MAC addresses are physical hardware identifiers, unlike IPs which are logically assigned.

questions.question: The ______ protocol is connection-oriented and uses a 3-way handshake.
questions.explanation: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) is famous for its reliability and handshake process.

questions.question: What port is typically used for SSH (Secure Shell)?
questions.explanation: Port 22 is the standard for SSH, which is used for secure remote command-line access.

questions.question: Which protocol would be best for a live video stream where speed is more important than small losses?
questions.explanation: UDP is preferred for real-time traffic because it doesn't wait to re-send lost data, keeping the stream 'live'.

questions.question: IPv6 addresses are much longer than IPv4 addresses. How many bits are in an IPv6 address?
questions.explanation: IPv4 uses 32 bits (~4 billion addresses), while IPv6 uses 128 bits (virtually infinite addresses).

questions.question: A 'Layer 2' device that connects computers within the same local network is called a:
questions.explanation: Switches operate at Layer 2 (Data Link) and use MAC addresses to forward data within a LAN.

questions.question: DNS (Domain Name System) converts IP addresses into human-readable domain names.
questions.explanation: Correct. It functions like a phonebook, mapping 'google.com' to an IP like '142.250.xxx.xxx'.

questions.question: What is the correct order of the 3-way handshake in TCP?
questions.explanation: SYN (Synchronize), SYN-ACK (Synchronize-Acknowledge), and ACK (Acknowledge) is the standard flow.

questions.question: A packet arrives at your router with a Destination Port of 80. What service is this packet likely for?
questions.explanation: Port 80 is the standard port for HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol), which is unencrypted web traffic.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model handles encryption and data compression?
questions.explanation: The Presentation layer (Layer 6) is responsible for formatting data, including encryption.

questions.question: The ______ address is required for a computer to communicate on the internet beyond its local router.
questions.explanation: IP addresses provide the logical routing needed to cross different networks on the internet.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid IPv4 address?
questions.explanation: Valid IPv4 octets range from 0 to 255. 172.16.254.1 is valid; 500 is out of range.

questions.question: UDP is 'connectionless', meaning it doesn't establish a formal link before sending data.
questions.explanation: UDP just starts sending data without waiting for a confirmation from the receiver.

questions.question: DNS runs on which port by default?
questions.explanation: Port 53 is the standard port for DNS queries.

questions.question: Which OSI layer is often referred to as 'The Bit Layer' because it deals with raw electrical signals?
questions.explanation: The Physical layer (Layer 1) deals with bits, wires, and signals.

questions.question: A hacker uses a tool to see which ports are 'Open' or 'Closed'. This activity is called port ______.
questions.explanation: Port scanning (e.g., using Nmap) is a key reconnaissance technique.

questions.question: If you want to transfer a file manually using a legacy, unencrypted method, you would use Port 21 (______).
questions.explanation: FTP (File Transfer Protocol) uses Port 21 for control and Port 20 for data transfer.

questions.question: Routers use MAC addresses to decide where to send packets across the internet.
questions.explanation: False. Routers use IP addresses (Layer 3) to route data between different networks.

questions.question: What does the mnemonic 'Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away' represent?
options: The layers of the OSI model from bottom to top
The components of a secure password
The steps of the hacking lifecycle
questions.explanation: P-D-N-T-S-P-A corresponds to Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.
